metric_value: End of support within 18 months
notes: Aberdeen predicts Sybase will actively encourage users off 4.9 and cut back support within 18 months of 1997 publication.

metric_value: Less than 4% of $13B RDBMS market
notes: Sybase's RDBMS market share fell below 4% by 2002 (IDC data per InformationWeek). 4.9 support was discontinued as predicted.

metric_value: Financial difficulties may threaten long-term viability
notes: Aberdeen notes Sybase's recent financial losses raise concerns about long-term product support.

metric_value: Acquired by SAP for $5.8 billion in July 2010
notes: Sybase survived as independent entity until 2010; was profitable niche player in mobile enterprise. Acquired by SAP, brand retired 2014.

metric_value: Strong candidate to be a long-term RDBMS market player
notes: Aberdeen cites VAR strengths and revenue growth as signs Informix will be among 2-3 surviving RDBMS vendors.

metric_value: Informix database sold to IBM for $1 billion in 2001 after accounting fraud scandal
notes: 1997 accounting fraud under CEO Phillip White led to restatements. Database unit sold to IBM April 2001. Remaining entity renamed Ascential Software, acquired by IBM 2005.

metric_value: 25 DataBlade modules available
notes: Aberdeen reports 25 DataBlade modules at time of study covering text, video, ROLAP, spatial, time series, and others.

metric_value: Informix-OnLine provides superior SMP scalability vs. Sybase System 11
notes: Informix-OnLine DS 6.0/7.0 provide kernel-level SMP scalability. Sybase System 11 limited to 4 processors at time.

metric_value: Informix-OnLine supports row-level locking; Sybase System 11 does not
notes: Enables SAP and other major OLTP application support. Key differentiator cited by Aberdeen.

metric_value: Migration to System 11 requires hardware expansion, stored procedure rewrites, administrator retraining
notes: Aberdeen documents multi-step migration: preparation, upgrade, followup — all requiring significant IS effort.

metric_value: Informix migration requires few changes due to compatibility tooling
notes: Stored procedure parser, DataBlade API shims, DB-Lib converter (planned 2Q97) minimize migration effort.

metric_value: DB-Lib converter planned for 2Q97 release
notes: Informix announced DB-Lib converter to allow Sybase 4.9 client apps to run against Informix without code changes.

metric_value: 85% of Sybase users expected on System 11 within one year
notes: Sybase's own projection cited by Aberdeen. Reflects urgency to migrate users off 4.9.

metric_value: Web servers and data marts must scale beyond 8-processor SMP limits
notes: Aberdeen notes rapid scaling demands of internet and data mart workloads driving need for better SMP support.

metric_value: RDBMS market will support only 2-3 major players long-term
notes: Aberdeen forecasts market consolidation. Outcome: Oracle, Microsoft SQL Server, IBM DB2 are the dominant players today. Informix and Sybase both failed to remain independent.

metric_value: Oracle, Microsoft SQL Server, IBM DB2/PostgreSQL dominate; Informix and Sybase acquired
notes: Aberdeen's 2-3 player prediction was accurate. Neither Informix nor Sybase remained independent RDBMS vendors.

metric_value: Informix-OnLine XPS supports parallel scans, sorts, aggregates, joins, index builds, backup/restore
notes: Version 8.0 XPS extends parallel scalability to clustered/MPP environments.

metric_value: Informix Universal Server combines RDBMS with object-relational DataBlade extensions
notes: Combines Informix-OnLine with Illustra's technology. Supports user-defined data types, multimedia data, complex functions.

metric_value: Informix attractive to Internet providers as web database server
notes: Aberdeen notes partnerships for Internet toolkits including INFORMIX-NewEra on Web servers.

metric_value: Informix-OnLine supports three asynchronous replication types: high-availability, discrete, continuous
notes: Enterprise replication allows mass copy to data marts without impacting OLTP performance.

metric_value: Informix-OnLine 8.0 merges admin with Tivoli network management via SNMP
notes: Integration allows remote management of distributed database from single console.

metric_value: Informix has strong VAR channel for mid-market customers
notes: Aberdeen highlights VAR relationships as key advantage for medium-scale businesses. Distinguishes Informix from more expensive direct-sold RDBMSs.

metric_value: Informix accounting fraud scandal in 1997; CEO Phillip White later convicted
notes: In 1997, Informix was charged with accounting fraud and illegal insider trading. CEO Phillip White pleaded guilty to securities fraud in 2003 and was sentenced to 2 months in prison.
